title: Why I Want to Become an AI Engineer
excerpt: My journey into Computer Science started with a simple curiosity — how do machines learn? Here's my story.
content:
# Why I Want to Become an AI Engineer

My journey into Computer Science started with a simple curiosity — how do machines learn?

Growing up in Khulna, Bangladesh, I didn't have access to fancy computers or high-speed internet. But I had books, and I had questions. The first time I read about neural networks — about how a computer could recognize a cat in a photo — something clicked inside me.

## The Spark

It was during my first year at North Western University when I truly understood the power of AI. I wrote my first Python program, a simple number guessing game, and felt the magic of making a machine do exactly what I wanted.

But I wanted more. I wanted to make machines that could *think*.

## The Path Ahead

Right now I'm learning the fundamentals — Python, mathematics for ML, data structures. Every day I try to code something new, even if it's small. The Coursera Machine Learning course has been incredibly helpful.

## Why AI?

Because AI is not just the future of technology — it's the future of everything. Healthcare, education, agriculture — every field will be transformed. And I want to be one of the people building those transformations.

The road is long, but every great journey starts with a single step. This blog is my way of documenting that journey.

*— M Rayhan*

title: Building My First Web App with Vanilla JS
excerpt: No frameworks, no build tools — just pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Here's what I learned building the NWU Cover Page Generator.
content:
# Building My First Web App with Vanilla JS

When I decided to build the NWU Assignment Cover Page Generator, I made a deliberate choice: **no frameworks**. No React, no Vue, no jQuery. Just pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

Was it harder? Yes. Was it worth it? Absolutely.

## Why Vanilla JS?

Frameworks are amazing, but they can hide what's actually happening under the hood. I wanted to understand the DOM, event handling, and CSS manipulation from the ground up before adding abstraction layers.

## What I Built

The Cover Page Generator allows students at North Western University to generate properly formatted assignment cover pages. Users can:

- Enter their name, ID, and course details
- Select their department
- Preview the cover page in real time
- Print or save as PDF

## Key Lessons

**1. CSS Custom Properties are powerful.** Being able to change an entire theme by updating one variable is incredible.

**2. Event delegation saves performance.** Instead of adding listeners to every element, I learned to listen at the parent level.

**3. LocalStorage is your friend.** Saving user preferences without a backend felt like magic the first time.

## What's Next

I'm now working on my portfolio site — also vanilla JS, but much more complex. A full SPA with routing, API calls, and a PWA. The skills I built here are the foundation.

Check out the Cover Page Generator at [rayhan.iam.bd/cover](https://rayhan.iam.bd/cover).
